keyword: what to wear to a job interview men
title: What to Wear to a Job Interview: Men's Complete Outfit Guide
metaDesc: Not sure what to wear to a job interview? Our men's guide covers every dress code — from corporate to casual — with specific outfit tips.
h1: What to Wear to a Job Interview: The Complete Men's Guide
introHtml: First impressions are made in seconds, and what you wear to a job interview speaks before you even say a word. Whether you're walking into a law firm boardroom or a tech startup's open-plan office, dressing appropriately signals professionalism, self-awareness, and respect for the opportunity. This guide breaks down exactly what men should wear to a job interview — by dress code, industry, and occasion — so you can walk in feeling confident and polished.
bodyHtml:
Read the Dress Code Before You Dress Up

The golden rule of interview dressing is to dress one level above the company's everyday standard. Research the company's culture through their website, LinkedIn, and Glassdoor reviews. When in doubt, it's always better to be slightly overdressed than underdressed. Here's how to decode the most common dress codes:

Formal / Business Professional Interviews

Ideal for finance, law, consulting, and corporate environments. This is the classic power-dressing territory.

  • Suit: A well-fitted two-piece suit in navy, charcoal grey, or dark grey wool or wool-blend fabric.
  • Shirt: A crisp, plain white or pale blue cotton dress shirt — always ironed.
  • Tie: A silk tie in a subtle pattern (stripes, micro-dots) in a complementary colour like burgundy, navy, or silver.
  • Shoes: Oxford or Derby leather shoes in black or dark brown, polished to a shine.
  • Belt: Matching leather belt in the same tone as your shoes.
  • Socks: Dark, thin-knit socks that match your trouser colour — no ankle socks.

Business Casual Interviews

Common in marketing, retail management, media, and mid-size companies. Smart but not stiff.

  • Trousers: Tailored chinos or dress trousers in navy, stone, or grey.
  • Shirt: A button-down Oxford shirt or a fine-knit polo in white, light blue, or pale pink.
  • Blazer: A structured blazer in navy or mid-grey adds instant polish — even without a tie.
  • Shoes: Leather loafers, clean white leather sneakers (premium brands only), or Derby shoes in tan or brown.
  • Outerwear: A wool overcoat or a clean, structured mac in neutral tones if the weather calls for it.

Smart Casual Interviews

Typical for tech, creative agencies, startups, and design studios. You have more freedom, but "casual" never means sloppy.

  • Trousers: Dark slim-fit jeans (no rips) or tailored chinos in earth tones.
  • Top: A well-fitted crewneck or V-neck sweater in merino wool, or a clean OCBD shirt worn untucked with the collar open.
  • Shoes: Clean minimalist leather or suede sneakers, Chelsea boots, or loafers.
  • Layering: A neat bomber jacket or a structured overshirt works well in place of a blazer.

Key Grooming & Finishing Touches

Your outfit is only part of the picture. These details complete the look:

  • Ensure clothes are clean, pressed, and free of lint or pet hair.
  • Keep fragrance subtle — one or two sprays maximum.
  • Trim or style facial hair neatly the night before.
  • Keep accessories minimal: a classic watch, simple cufflinks, or a pocket square if wearing a suit.
  • Carry a clean, structured bag or portfolio folder — avoid backpacks for formal settings.

What to Avoid at Any Job Interview

  • Overly bright or loud colours and busy prints that distract.
  • Ill-fitting clothes — too baggy or too tight both undermine your image.
  • Scuffed, dirty, or worn-out shoes.
  • Visible logos or branded sportswear.
  • Casual footwear like flip-flops, sliders, or heavily worn trainers.

faq.q: Should I wear a suit to every job interview?
faq.a: Not necessarily. A suit is ideal for formal industries like finance, law, or consulting. For creative or tech roles, business casual or smart casual outfits are more appropriate and can actually show better cultural awareness. The key is to dress one level above the company's daily standard.

faq.q: Can men wear a blazer without a tie to a job interview?
faq.a: Yes — a blazer worn without a tie over a well-fitted dress shirt or Oxford button-down is a sharp, modern look for business casual interviews. Make sure the shirt collar lies flat and the blazer fits well at the shoulders.

faq.q: What colour suit is best for a job interview?
faq.a: Navy blue and charcoal grey are the safest and most authoritative choices for interview suits. They project confidence and professionalism without being overpowering. Avoid black suits unless the role or company culture specifically calls for it.

faq.q: Are clean white sneakers acceptable for a job interview?
faq.a: In smart casual or creative environments, yes — but only if they are premium, minimalist leather sneakers (such as those by Common Projects or similar brands) in pristine condition. Avoid chunky trainers, sports shoes, or any footwear with visible wear and tear.
